title: podman top hpid with cgroup v2 fails with "Error: couldn't find pids group for PID"
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

podman top $cid hpid fails with "Error: couldn't find pids group for PID"

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman run --rm --name test fedora sleep inf

2. podman top test hpid

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Error: couldn't find pids group for PID 2562
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
HPID
2562
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

This doesn't happen with version 1.5.1

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.1
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.6.1-2.fc31.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Silverblue 31 physical host

comments.body: What was the command line the container was started with? Any chance it was `--pid=host` / rootless / `--cgroups=disable`
The container was started by a regular user rootless.

Rootless doesn't have cgroups, so no PID cgroup, so this looks expected. We should improve documentation and errors for `top` to indicate that we don't expect some parts of it to work as rootless on CGroups v1.
Podman does find the correct pid, but the command now fails (and output the pid as the last word instead of within new line). How are we supposed to get the host pid of a container with CGroups v2?
We can get the PID of the first process in the container (it'll pop up in `inspect`), but without the PID cgroup we can't track anything beyond that first PID.

IIUC podman does create the PID cgroup:

```
[fedora@localhost ~]$ podman run --detach --rm --name test fedora bash -c "sleep inf & sleep infinity & sleep 3600"
2b035dd16cd88d100b9482e6f9b91a3031a36c95d0de7170916eca2a9b8dac9a

[fedora@localhost ~]$ podman exec test cat /proc/self/cgroup
0::/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/user.slice/libpod-2b035dd16cd88d100b9482e6f9b91a3031a36c95d0de7170916eca2a9b8dac9a.scope

[fedora@localhost ~]$ cat /sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/user.slice/libpod-2b035dd16cd88d100b9482e6f9b91a3031a36c95d0de7170916eca2a9b8dac9a.scope/cgroup.controllers 
memory pids

[fedora@localhost ~]$ systemd-cgls /user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/user.slice/libpod-2b035dd16cd88d100b9482e6f9b91a3031a36c95d0de7170916eca2a9b8dac9a.scope
Control group /user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/u>
├─2405 bash -c sleep inf & sleep infinity & sleep 3600
├─2410 sleep inf
├─2411 sleep infinity
└─2412 sleep 3600
```

Is this a V2 system? V2 with the systemd cgroup driver will create the PID cgroup.

@vrothberg I know we did some work to get psgo supporting V2. This error looks like it's out of psgo, though, which makes me suspect that we don't have full support yet?
This is a fedora 31 which seems to use cgroup v2 by default.
Actually this is already fixed in master, probably by https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4197
Yes we found this issue earlier, so the problem should be fixed when we release podman 1.6.2 or podman 1.7.
